target.name: Hugging Face

methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $8.6B.

data.narrative:
Hugging Face is a Series D AI & Software company currently valued at $4.5B post-money. The comparable universe consists of one public peer (PLTR) and eight private companies in the same sector and stage, including Wayve ($8.6B), Cohere ($7.0B), ElevenLabs ($3.0B), Hive AI ($2.0B), and Labelbox ($1.0B).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $4.5B last post-money valuation with a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for Series D advancement, implied a value of $6.8B. Peer-median analysis across the five identified same-stage comparables yielded a median valuation of $3.0B, with the peer set spanning $1.0B to $8.6B. The divergence between methods reflects meaningful dispersion within the frontier-tech AI cohort.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $2.5B to $7.8B, with a median implied value of $4.9B. The current $4.5B valuation sits within this range, near the lower-median boundary. Key uncertainties include the appropriate step-up multiplier for this stage (1.5x may understate market conditions for differentiated AI platforms) and the relevance of the peer set given Hugging Face's distinct position in model distribution and community infrastructure. Additional revenue, margin, and growth trajectory data would sharpen the range.
